title: Pakistan Formally Rejects UN Experts' Claim of 'No Credible Evidence' for TTP Attacks from Afghan Soil
detail: Pakistan's government issued a detailed official statement formally rejecting UN human rights experts' claim that there was 'no credible evidence' TTP attacks inside Pakistan were directed or controlled from Afghan territory. Pakistan cited multiple authoritative sources in its rebuttal: the 35th, 36th, and 37th UN Security Council Monitoring Team reports documenting TTP presence in Afghanistan; SIGAR assessments documenting 13,000–23,000 foreign militants in Afghanistan including 6,000+ TTP fighters; over 600 documented TTP attacks launched from Afghan soil; and assessments from Russia, the CSTO, and Shanghai Cooperation Organization. Pakistan questioned what additional evidence the UN experts required, characterizing their position as politically motivated and inconsistent with UN Security Council monitoring team findings. The statement significantly raised diplomatic stakes at the Urumqi talks.

title: Pakistan Announces Cumulative Operation Toll: 796 Taliban Killed, 286 Checkposts Destroyed
detail: Pakistan's Information Minister Attaullah Tarar announced updated cumulative operational figures for Operation Ghazab-il-Haq as of April 5: 796 Afghan Taliban fighters killed and 1,043+ wounded since the operation's launch. 286 Taliban checkposts had been destroyed and 44 captured. Pakistan claimed 249 tanks, armored vehicles, artillery guns, and drones had been neutralized. A total of 81 locations across Afghanistan had been targeted by airstrikes since the operation began in late February. Afghan Taliban officials disputed the figures, asserting Pakistan's casualty claims were massively inflated and that actual military losses were far lower, while civilian casualties were far higher than Pakistan admitted.

media.caption: Pakistan announces 796 Taliban killed, 286 checkposts destroyed in Operation Ghazab-il-Haq
